query: List all walls and partitions located on Level 1
description: Broad architectural query — baseline should partially succeed

query: What doors are installed on Level 2?
description: Specific element type — Level 2

query: What is present on Level 2? Give me the complete inventory.
description: Exhaustive inventory — dense RAG mathematically cannot list all elements

query: List every single element on Level 1. Do not omit anything.
description: Explicit exhaustive demand — hardest for semantic search

query: What HVAC equipment is on Level 2?
description: MEP query — tests IFC type synonym expansion

query: List all mechanical and plumbing assets on Level 1
description: MEP inventory — should trigger MEP filter in graph_query node

query: Are there any pumps or fans on Level 2?
description: Targeted MEP query — tests IfcPump and IfcFan retrieval

query: What electrical fixtures are installed on Level 1?
description: Electrical equipment query — tests IfcLightFixture and IfcOutlet

query: Which floors contain doors and how many are on each floor?
description: Cross-floor aggregation — no spatial constraint, should use dense retrieval

query: How many walls does the building have in total across all floors?
description: Building-wide aggregation — spatial constraint absent

query: Which floor has the most elements?
description: Comparative cross-floor — requires counting per storey

query: Show me all elements on the foundation level
description: Non-standard floor name — tests storey name matching robustness

query: What is on the roof?
description: Ambiguous floor name — 'roof' vs 'Roof' case sensitivity test

query: List all elements on the upper level
description: Adversarial: informal floor name — LLM must map 'upper level' to 'Level 2'

query: What is on the ground floor?
description: Adversarial: 'ground floor' maps to Level 1 in most buildings

query: List all IfcBoiler elements on Level 1
description: Adversarial: element type absent — system must return empty, not hallucinate

query: What nuclear reactors are installed on Level 2?
description: Adversarial: impossible element — hallucination trap, correct answer is 'none found'

query: Show me everything that is NOT on Level 1
description: Adversarial: negation query — current pipeline cannot handle negation correctly

query: What elements are on floors 1 and 2 combined?
description: Adversarial: multi-floor single query — pipeline extracts only one floor constraint

query: List all walls on the ground floor
description: Wall query on German IFC file — tests multi-file isolation and non-English storey names

query: What is on the upper floor of the FZK house?
description: Inventory on German IFC — tests LLM mapping of 'upper floor' to Dachgeschoss

query: List every element on the ground floor of the institute building
description: Inventory on large German IFC — 205 elements, highest latency expected

query: What windows exist on Level 2?
description: IfcWindow query — tests architectural element retrieval

query: Find all sensors and actuators in the building
description: Building-wide BMS query — no floor constraint, tests cross-floor MEP retrieval

query: What slabs are on Level 2?
description: IfcSlab query — structural element, should be present on Level 2

query: What HVAC equipment is on the ground floor?
description: MEP query on PCERT-HVAC file — IFC4, real IfcAirTerminal + IfcDuctSegment present, F1 should be >0

query: List all air terminals on the ground floor
description: Targeted MEP query — 2 IfcAirTerminal elements confirmed in oracle. Tests precision on exact type match.

query: What walls are on the ground floor of the office building?
description: Wall query on IFC4 PCERT architectural model — validates IFC4 schema support

query: List all structural elements on the ground floor
description: Structural query on PCERT structural model — tests IfcFooting and mixed structural type retrieval

query: Are there any ducts or ductwork on the ground floor?
description: Duct query on PCERT-HVAC — 1 IfcDuctSegment confirmed. Tests synonym expansion for 'ductwork'.
